title.en: Rename the Native Token from Toncoin (TON) to Gram (GRAM)

description.en:
## Summary Telegram proposes renaming the native token of The Open Network from Toncoin to Gram, and changing its ticker from TON to GRAM. This is a name-and-ticker change only. The blockchain is not renamed – it remains TON / The Open Network. There is no swap, migration, bridge, claim, or conversion. Every balance, address, contract, and position stays exactly as it is today. A balance of 10 TON simply becomes 10 GRAM. ## Motivation As part of the broader MTONGA (Make TON Great Again) initiative, we propose returning the native token to its original name. In the original TON White Paper, the network's native token was called Gram. It's more than a rename; it's a return to the roots. Gram is the name the network was born with, and in truth it never fully left: it's still used extensively throughout the core TON codebase (see the references here). Adopting it as the token's public name simply aligns the user-facing brand with the protocol's founding documentation and intent. The change is not a technical update to the blockchain. The blockchain keeps the name TON / The Open Network. Only the native token's name (Toncoin → Gram) and ticker (TON → GRAM) change. ## Specification – what changes, what doesn't

Native token name: Toncoin → Gram

Ticker: TON → GRAM

Blockchain / network name: Unchanged — remains TON / The Open Network

Role as native asset: Unchanged

Balances: Unchanged — e.g. 10 TON becomes 10 GRAM

Gas fees: Still paid in the native unit, now displayed as GRAM

Addresses: Unchanged

Smart contracts: Unchanged — continue to work as before

Validators / staking: Unchanged — positions displayed as GRAM

NFTs & jettons: Unchanged

DeFi positions: Unchanged (lending, liquidity, staking, borrowing, etc.)

CEX balances: Unchanged — exchanges update name/ticker on their own schedule

Swap / bridge / claim / migration: None — no action required from users

## Timeline The full process is designed to complete within 3 weeks from the start of voting. ### Voting

Duration: Week 1 (7 days)

The community votes on TON Vote.

### Rollout

Duration: Weeks 2–3 (14 days)

If approved, all platforms — wallets, explorers, exchanges, and partners — update the display name from Toncoin to Gram and ticker from TON to GRAM.

If the vote passes, all platforms are expected to complete the transition within two weeks of the voting close, so that within three weeks of the vote opening, the name and ticker are updated consistently across the ecosystem — wallets, explorers, exchanges, and partners.

## Voting Process We propose the following sequence of actions and timelines:
  • The vote runs for one week. Voting power is weighted by the TON balances at the end of the voting process.
  • The vote runs on the smart contracts of one of the largest open DAO platforms on TON — TON Vote.

Options: For / Against / Abstain

## FAQ What is happening?

The native token Toncoin is being renamed to Gram, and the ticker is changing from TON to GRAM.

Is the TON blockchain being renamed?

No. The blockchain remains TON / The Open Network.

Is Gram a new token?

No. Gram is simply the new name and ticker for the existing native token previously known as Toncoin.

Do I need to swap my TON?

No. There is no swap, migration, bridge, claim, or conversion of any kind.

What happens to my balance?

It stays the same. 10 TON before the rename is 10 GRAM after the rename.

Are smart contracts affected?

No. Existing contracts continue to work exactly as before.

Are staking positions affected?

No. They remain unchanged and will simply be displayed as GRAM.

Are NFTs affected?

No. NFTs are unaffected.

Are DeFi positions affected?

No. Lending, liquidity, staking, borrowing, and other positions are unaffected.

Are exchange (CEX) balances affected?

No. Exchanges will update the display name and ticker on their own rollout schedule. Your balance does not change.

What happens to gas fees?

Gas continues to be paid in the native token of The Open Network — now displayed as GRAM.

What is TON after the rename?

TON remains the name of the blockchain and ecosystem. GRAM is the native token of TON.

How do I avoid scams?

Do not use any website, bot, or link that asks you to claim, swap, migrate, or convert TON to GRAM. No action is required from you. This is a display-name change only.

## ⚠️ Security Note This is a name change only. No one needs to move, swap, claim, bridge, or convert anything. Any site, bot, or message instructing you to "migrate TON to GRAM" or "claim your GRAM" is a scam. Your funds are safe and require no action.
